title: Mesosphaerum hilarii
intro: Mesosphaerum hilarii, also known as Hilair's hyptis or Brazilian hyptis, is a plant species in the mint family that is native to Brazil. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Mesosphaerum hilarii include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mesosphaerum hilarii is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, or used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for respiratory issues and other health concerns.
taxonomy: Mesosphaerum hilarii is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The classification of Mesosphaerum hilarii includes its kingdom as Plantae, and its phylum as Angiospermophyta. It is classified under the order Lamiales, and its family is Lamiaceae. This plant's classification also includes its class as Magnoliopsida and its order as Lamiales.

Mesosphaerum hilarii has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
